org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems

Class Drive

  • java.lang.Object
    • SubsystemBase
      • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.Drive

  • public class Drive
extends SubsystemBase
    Main drive subsystem that integrates swerve drivetrain control with PedroPathing autonomous navigation.

    This subsystem serves as the primary interface for all robot motion control. It combines two key components:

    • PedroPathing Follower: Handles autonomous path following, pose estimation, and field-centric navigation during autonomous
    • OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2: Manages the physical swerve drive modules with flexible encoder support (OctoQuad digital or SRS Hub analog)

    Encoder Flexibility:

    This drivetrain supports two encoder types, selectable via Constants.SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE:

    • OctoQuad (Default): High-precision digital encoders with 8192 CPR
    • Analog (SRS Hub): Potentiometers or magnetic encoders via SRS Hub analog inputs

    Switch between encoder types by changing one constant - no code changes needed!

    IMU Configuration:

    REV Hub IMU is DISABLED to reduce I2C bus traffic and improve loop times.

    • Disabled: REV Hub IMU (Control Hub/Expansion Hub built-in IMU)
    • Primary Heading Source: GoBilda Pinpoint IMU (used for PedroPathing odometry)
    • Optional Heading Source: OctoQuad IMU (when LOCALIZER_ENABLED is true)

    Performance: By disabling the REV Hub IMU and using only the GoBilda Pinpoint IMU, we reduce I2C bus contention and improve loop times. The Pinpoint IMU is accessed via its own I2C bus and provides reliable heading data.

    Sensor Fusion:

    When enabled, this subsystem uses an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) to fuse three localization sources for maximum accuracy:

    1. Swerve Odometry: High-frequency updates from drive wheel encoders
    2. Pinpoint Odometry: High-accuracy translation from deadwheel encoders
    3. Limelight Vision: Global position correction from AprilTags

    Current Management:

    Includes slew rate limiting and current limiting to prevent 20A fuse trips:

    • Slew Rate Limiting: Prevents rapid power changes that cause current spikes
    • Current Limiting: Scales motor power when Floodgage sensor detects high current

    Operation Modes:

    The drive subsystem supports three operation modes:

    1. Field-Centric TeleOp: Driver control relative to the field. Robot movement stays consistent regardless of robot heading. Best for game piece manipulation.
    2. Robot-Centric TeleOp: Driver control relative to the robot. Forward always moves the robot in the direction it's facing. Best for fine positioning.
    3. Autonomous Path Following: Automatic navigation along pre-generated paths using PedroPathing trajectory following.

    Coordinate Systems:

    • Field Coordinates: X = forward/back (inches), Y = left/right (inches), Heading = rotation (radians, 0 = facing red alliance wall)
    • Chassis Speeds: VX = forward velocity, VY = lateral velocity, Omega = rotational velocity (radians/sec)

    Telemetry & Logging:

    This subsystem provides comprehensive telemetry output:

    • FTC Dashboard field overlay with robot position and wheel orientations
    • Encoder type display (OctoQuad or Analog)
    • CSV logging of pose, velocity, and module states for post-match analysis
    • Real-time target vs actual motor commands for tuning
    See Also:
    com.pedropathing.follower.Follower, OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2, SwerveEncoder,

    • Field Detail

      • robot

        private final Robot robot
        Reference to the robot singleton for accessing hardware and telemetry.

      • follower

        public final Follower follower
        The PedroPathing follower instance for autonomous navigation. Handles path following, pose estimation, and localization. Integrates with odometry (Pinpoint) and vision (Limelight) for pose updates.

      • swerve

        public final OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2 swerve
        The physical swerve drivetrain controller. Manages four swerve modules with PIDF control for steering and drive motors. Translates chassis speeds into individual module commands via inverse kinematics.

        Supports both OctoQuad digital encoders and SRS Hub analog encoders. Switch via Constants.SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE.

      • sensorFusion

        private final SensorFusionLocalizer sensorFusion
        Sensor fusion localizer for combining Pinpoint, swerve odometry, and Limelight. Uses Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) for optimal pose estimation.

      • swerveOdometry

        private final SwerveOdometry swerveOdometry
        Swerve odometry tracker using drive wheel encoders. Provides high-frequency pose updates for the EKF prediction step.

      • pinpointHealthMonitor

        private final PinpointHealthMonitor pinpointHealthMonitor
        Health monitor for detecting Pinpoint drift. Automatically disables Pinpoint corrections when it's drifted too far from truth.

      • visionTagWasVisible

        private boolean visionTagWasVisible
        Tracks whether vision tag was visible in the previous loop. Used for Pinpoint auto-reset logic.

      • teleOpSpeeds

        private ChassisSpeeds teleOpSpeeds
        Cached TeleOp chassis speeds for current limiting in periodic(). Stores the most recent driver input from setTeleOpDrive().

      • isTeleOpMode

        private boolean isTeleOpMode
        Whether TeleOp drive is active (true) or autonomous is active (false). Used to determine which chassis speeds to apply in periodic().

      • loopStartTime

        private double loopStartTime
        Tracks loop timing for performance monitoring.

      • lastLoopTime

        private double lastLoopTime

      • currentLimitingActive

        private boolean currentLimitingActive
        Tracks whether current limiting is actively reducing motor power.

      • batteryVoltage

        private double batteryVoltage
        Tracks battery voltage for power monitoring.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Drive

        public Drive()
        Constructs the Drive subsystem and initializes all components.

        This constructor:

        1. Creates the PedroPathing follower with configured constants
        2. Initializes the OctoSwerveDrivetrain with motors, servos, and PIDF values
        3. Configures the drivetrain with robot physical dimensions

        Hardware Requirements: Requires the following to be initialized in Robot:

        • 4x drive motors (MotorEx)
        • 4x steering servos (CRServoEx)
        • 1x OctoQuad encoder processor

    • Method Detail

      • init

        public void init()
        Initializes the drive subsystem for operation.

        This method must be called before the drive subsystem is used. It performs PedroPathing initialization and sets the robot's starting pose.

        Starting Pose: Defaults to (0, 0, 0) which represents:

        • X = 0 inches (starting position)
        • Y = 0 inches (starting position)
        • Heading = 0 radians (facing the red alliance wall)

        Custom Starting Pose: To start at a different position (e.g., for autonomous from different starting tiles), call: 

        
 follower.setStartingPose(new Pose(startX, startY, startHeading));

      • configureSensorFusionNoise

        private void configureSensorFusionNoise()
        Configures the sensor fusion noise parameters from Constants.

        This method sets up the EKF noise covariance matrices based on the sensor characteristics. Lower noise = more trust in that sensor.

      • getSensorFusion

        public SensorFusionLocalizer getSensorFusion()
        Gets the sensor fusion localizer.

        This provides access to the EKF for external subsystems (like Vision) to apply pose corrections.

        Returns:
        the sensor fusion localizer

      • getSwerveOdometry

        public SwerveOdometry getSwerveOdometry()
        Gets the swerve odometry tracker.
        Returns:
        the swerve odometry tracker

      • getTeleOpSpeeds

        public ChassisSpeeds getTeleOpSpeeds()
        Gets the current TeleOp chassis speeds.
        Returns:
        the current TeleOp chassis speeds

      • setTeleOpDrive

        public void setTeleOpDrive(double forward,
                           double lateral,
                           double turn,
                           boolean isFieldCentric)
        Sets the drivetrain velocities for manual TeleOp control.

        This method accepts driver input and converts it into chassis speeds for the swerve drivetrain. Supports both field-centric and robot-centric driving modes.

        Field-Centric Drive:

        When isFieldCentric = true, the driver's input is interpreted relative to the field rather than the robot. This provides intuitive control:

        • Forward always moves the robot away from the driver
        • Left always moves the robot to the driver's left
        • Turning rotates the robot in place

        Math: Field-centric drive applies a rotation matrix to the input based on the robot's current heading from the PedroPathing localizer: 

         rotX = forward * cos(-heading) - lateral * sin(-heading)
 rotY = forward * sin(-heading) + lateral * cos(-heading)

        Robot-Centric Drive:

        When isFieldCentric = false, the driver's input is interpreted relative to the robot's current orientation:

        • Forward moves in the direction the robot is facing
        • Left moves perpendicular to the robot's heading
        • Turn rotates the robot

        Current Limiting:

        The chassis speeds are stored and applied with current limiting in the periodic() loop. This ensures current limiting is applied consistently in both TeleOp and auto modes.

        Input Range:

        All inputs should be in the range [-1.0, 1.0]. The method will scale these to the maximum drive velocity configured in Constants.

        Parameters:
        forward - forward/backward velocity [-1.0, 1.0]. Positive = forward.
        lateral - left/right velocity [-1.0, 1.0]. Positive = left.
        turn - rotational velocity [-1.0, 1.0]. Positive = counter-clockwise.
        isFieldCentric - if true, input is field-centric; if false, input is robot-centric
        See Also:
        ChassisSpeeds, OctoSwerveDrivetrain#drive(ChassisSpeeds, double)

      • periodic

        public void periodic()
        Periodic update method called every loop iteration by the command scheduler.

        This method is the main update loop for the drive subsystem. It performs the sensor fusion EKF cycle and applies drive commands:

        1. Sensor Fusion EKF Cycle

        Every loop iteration, the EKF fuses three localization sources:

        • Predict: Use swerve odometry for high-frequency pose prediction
        • Correct (Pinpoint): Apply deadwheel odometry corrections
        • Correct (Limelight): Applied by Vision subsystem when tags are visible

        2. Update PedroPathing with Fused Pose

        The fused pose estimate from the EKF is used to update PedroPathing's localizer, ensuring autonomous paths use the best available pose estimate.

        3. Drive Command Execution

        This method applies either TeleOp or autonomous chassis speeds with current limiting:

        • Autonomous: If PedroPathing follower is busy, extract desired velocities from the path follower and apply them with current limiting
        • TeleOp: If in TeleOp mode, apply the stored driver input with current limiting

        Current limiting reads from the SRS Hub Floodgate sensor and scales chassis speeds when approaching the 20A fuse limit to prevent trips.

        4. Telemetry & Visualization

        Sends telemetry data to FTC Dashboard and CSV logger:

        • Field Overlay: Draws robot box (18x18 inches), heading line, and wheel orientation indicators on the field
        • Telemetry: Sends target vs actual module angles and velocities
        • CSV Logging: Records robot pose, heading, and module states for post-match analysis

        Visualization Details:

        • Robot Box: Blue rectangle (#3F51B5) showing robot footprint
        • Heading Line: 9-inch line from robot center showing forward direction
        • Wheel Indicators: Orange 2.5-inch lines showing each wheel's actual steering angle (updates in real-time for tuning)

        Performance Note: This method runs approximately 50 times per second. Keep operations efficient to maintain loop timing under 20ms.
